verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass TM protein primarily localized to the ER lumen-facing side of the ER membrane. However, it exhibits documented partial plasma membrane localization via its own TM anchor, with bidirectional trafficking to ER-PM junctions upon Ca2+ store depletion. The surface pool is functional and directly accessible from the extracellular space via dual type I/II topology. Surface display is activation-induced (ER store depletion triggers translocation to ER-PM contact sites), qualifying as contextual via cell_state_induced.
